title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Gaborone City Council (dog Management) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Gaborone City Council (dog Management) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-laws may be cited as the Gaborone City Council (Dog Management) Bye-laws.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Issue of dog licence
provisions.content:
3. Issue of dog licence
(1) A person shall not keep a dog unless he or she has been issued a licence under these Bye-laws.
(2) The types of dog licences and the applicable fees shall be as set out in Schedule 2.
(3) An owner of a dog shall, within four weeks of a dog attaining the age of three months, apply to an authorising officer for a dog licence in Form A set out in Schedule 1.
(4) An authorised officer, shall, on receipt of an application under subbye-law (3) and upon payment of a fee set out in Schedule 2 and after the production of a valid vaccination certificate by the applicant, issue the applicant a dog licence in accordance with Form B set out in Schedule 1.
(5) A person who contravenes subbye-law (1) commits an offence and is liable to a fine not exceeding P500 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two months, or to both.

provisions.title: Validity of licence
provisions.content:
4. Validity of licence
(1) A licence issued under these Bye-laws shall be valid for an indefinite period, subject to payment of an annual licence fee as set out in Schedule 2, and compliance with the conditions upon which it is issued.
(2) A licensee shall not hire, cede, transfer or in any way whatsoever make over the licence to any other person.

provisions.title: Duplicate licence
provisions.content:
5. Duplicate licence
(1) Where a dog licence is lost, destroyed or mutilated, the Council shall upon application by a licensee in Form C set out in Schedule 1 and upon payment of the fee set out in Schedule 2, issue a duplicate licence.
(2) Where a licensee recovers a licence which has been lost and replaced, he or she shall return the duplicate licence to the Council.

provisions.title: Cancellation of licence
provisions.content:
6. Cancellation of licence
(1) The Council may at any time while a licence is in force, revoke the licence where the Council is satisfied that the keeping of a dog on the land or premises specified in the licence
( a ) has caused or materially contributed to a nuisance or the likelihood of an injury or loss of life; or
( b ) has led to a failure to comply with all or any of the terms and conditions of the licence.

definitions.definition: any person appointed as such by Gaborone City Council Town Clerk, BSPCA official or, authorised veterinary surgeon
Botswana Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals
the area within the jurisdiction of the Council
a dog aged three months and above
a person responsible for a dog, having temporary possession, charge or control over a dog
the seizure, taking into custody or receiving into custody, confinement or other exercise of control over a dog, by any authorised officer
a structure or shelter for dogs or any building or property in which dogs are housed, maintained or bred
a collar chain or harness of appropriate material, securely attached to a correctly fitted dog collar and of no more than six meters in length
a licence issued by the Council to a dog owner in terms of bye-law 3
any person to whom a licence has been issued under these Bye-laws
an encoded electronic device implanted on a dog by or under the supervision of a veterinary surgeon, which contains a unique code number and provides information of the owner
that a dog is not on a lead or leash
an area where a dog may be exercised whilst not on a leash or lead
any part of any street, road or public place where a dog shall be kept on a leash or lead at all times
that a dog is on a lead or leash which is held by a person, so that the dog cannot break free
any area into which a dog is not allowed entry
a guide dog, guard dog, breeding dog, sniffer dog, therapeutic dog, educational or entertainment dog
a veterinarian as defined in the Veterinary Surgeons Act (Cap. 61:04).
